value.title: Why BoltDB over Redis for the bundled KV
value.body: We chose BoltDB for the bundled KV store because the core product must be complete with zero external services: a single embedded file, transactional, crash-safe, no daemon to operate. Redis remains available as an overlay backend for fleet deployments where nodes must share state, but the default experience ships batteries-included. Decided 2026-03; revisit only if single-node write throughput becomes a measured bottleneck.

value.title: Why production runs in FRA
value.body: Production web-tier nodes run in Fly's FRA region because our measured p50 to both the primary Postgres (Neon eu-central) and the majority of early tenants is lowest from Frankfurt; CDG serves as the edge fallback. Moving compute closer to the database beat moving it closer to users for our write-heavy control-plane traffic. Decided 2026-07; revisit when US tenant traffic passes 30%.

value.title: Why the AI gateway streams byte-transparent
value.body: The AI-gateway inlet forwards upstream SSE bytes verbatim and never routes the stream through the rule engine: the stack runs once on the request, and the transport stays indistinguishable from talking to the provider directly. Policy that needs response data reads the completion envelope after the stream ends. This keeps every processor synchronous and makes client compatibility trivial.

value.title: Why SQLite runs in WAL mode
value.body: All bundled SQLite databases run in WAL mode with a busy_timeout DSN: readers never block the writer, and the dbcache reload uses the online-backup API instead of cache=shared (which produced SQLITE_LOCKED under concurrency). Cache=shared is explicitly forbidden; see the chassis DSN helpers.
